quotes: Caring is the essence of nursing. — Jean Watson
Nurses dispense comfort, compassion, and caring without even a prescription. — Val Saintsbury
To do what nobody else will do, in a way that nobody else can, in spite of all we go through — that is what it is to be a nurse. — Rawsi Williams
The trained nurse has become one of the great blessings of humanity. — William Osler
Where the needs of the world and your talents cross, there lies your vocation. — Aristotle
Not all of us can do great things. But we can do small things with great love. — Mother Teresa
They may forget your name, but they will never forget how you made them feel. — Maya Angelou
The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others. — Gandhi
Constant attention by a good nurse may be just as important as a major operation by a surgeon. — Dag Hammarskjold
When you're a nurse, you know that every day you will touch a life or a life will touch yours. — Unknown
Bound by paperwork, short on hands, sleep, and energy — nurses are rarely short on caring. — Sharon Hudacek
It would not be possible to praise nurses too highly. — Stephen Ambrose
Nursing is not for everyone. It takes a very strong, intelligent, and compassionate person to take on the toughest job there is. — Unknown
Be the nurse you would want as a patient. — Unknown
Every nurse was drawn to nursing because of a desire to care, to serve, or to help. — Christina Feist-Heilmeier
You treat a disease — you win, you lose. You treat a person, I guarantee you win. — Patch Adams
Courage is not the absence of fear, but rather the judgement that something else is more important. — Ambrose Redmoon
The character of the nurse is as important as the knowledge she possesses. — Carolyn Jarvis
Our job as nurses is to cushion the sorrow and celebrate the joy, every day, while we are just doing our jobs. — Christine Belle
Have a heart that never hardens, a temper that never tires, and a touch that never hurts. — Charles Dickens
I attribute my success to this: I never gave or took any excuse. — Florence Nightingale
Hardships often prepare ordinary people for an extraordinary destiny. — C.S. Lewis
The question is not how to survive, but how to thrive with passion, compassion, humour, and style. — Maya Angelou
Do what you can, with what you have, where you are. — Theodore Roosevelt
Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can. — Arthur Ashe
Aroha mai, aroha atu — love received, love given.
He aha te mea nui o te ao? He tangata, he tangata, he tangata. — What is the greatest thing? It is people.
Ehara taku toa i te toa takitahi, engari he toa takitini. — My strength is not mine alone, but the strength of many.
You're braver than you believe, stronger than you seem, and smarter than you think. — A.A. Milne
Medicine is a science of uncertainty and an art of probability. — William Osler
